title: polypodium vulgare subsp. serratum
intro: Polypodium vulgare subsp. serratum, also known as the serrated polypody, is a subspecies of the common polypody fern (Polypodium vulgare) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The serrated polypody has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive issues, due to its expectorant, anti-oxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
